reasoning: Mauritius is one of the most stable and peaceful countries in Africa. Historically, the base rate of 'Violence against civilians' is extremely low—around 6 events per year, making the 30-day average around 0.5. As of late May 2025, the baseline metric was 1.5. To exceed 10 times this baseline (i.e. > 15 events), Mauritius would need 16 or more 'Violence against civilians' events in a single 30-day window. This would represent an unprecedented internal crisis or conflict. As of early June 2025, there are no news reports indicating current uprisings, massive protests, or armed group formations in Mauritius. Because the current environment is very stable, the probability of such an extreme spike in violence in the next 1-6 months is incredibly low, bounded primarily by the 0.05 forecasting range floor.
